proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65, plausibly age in years) is positively associated with pfs_months across the cohort: each 1-unit increase in feature_080 corresponds to a longer pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy import stats; r, p = stats.pearsonr(df['feature_080'], df['pfs_months']); slope = r * df['pfs_months'].std()/df['feature_080'].std()
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r = +0.702 (slope ~ +0.176 mo per unit feature_080). Mean pfs_months by decade: 0.56 (<=40) -> 1.52 (40-50) -> 3.06 (50-60) -> 4.67 (60-70) -> 6.32 (70-80) -> 8.20 (80-90). Effect is monotonic and roughly linear. Hugely significant (p effectively 0).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (ordinal 0/1/2) is negatively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_063 levels (likely indicating greater disease severity, e.g., stage or grade) are associated with shorter pfs_months in a graded fashion.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months by feature_063: level 0 = 5.64 (n=17592), level 1 = 4.44 (n=24971), level 2 = 3.29 (n=7437). Slope ~ -1.18 mo per level (Pearson r = -0.32). Each level decreases pfs_months by approximately 1.18 months — graded prognostic effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among binary features, feature_056 (prevalence 30%) is associated with markedly shorter pfs_months: presence of feature_056 reduces mean pfs_months by approximately 1 to 2 months relative to its absence.
feature_042 (prevalence 35%) is associated with longer pfs_months: presence of feature_042 increases mean pfs_months by approximately 1 month relative to its absence.
feature_048 (prevalence 10%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: presence of feature_048 reduces mean pfs_months by about 1 month.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months: feature_056=1 vs 0 differs by -1.54 months (t-test p effectively 0). Strong negative prognostic association — likely a marker of disease severity.
Mean pfs_months: feature_042=1 vs 0 differs by +1.10 months (t-test p effectively 0). Strong positive association at the marginal level — candidate for therapeutic effect.
Mean pfs_months: feature_048=1 vs 0 differs by -1.05 months (p ~ 1.8e-179). Negative prognostic marker, lower prevalence (10%).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_111 (binary, prevalence 70%) is positively associated with pfs_months, while feature_015 (binary, prevalence 35%) and feature_040 (binary, prevalence 18%) are negatively associated; all of these effects remain after multivariable adjustment for age (feature_080), feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, and feature_048.

analyses.code: import statsmodels.api as sm; X = sm.add_constant(df[['feature_080','feature_063','feature_056','feature_042','feature_048','feature_015','feature_040','feature_111']]); sm.OLS(df['pfs_months'], X).fit().summary()
analyses.result_summary: In a 13-variable OLS model: feature_080 +0.176/yr, feature_063 -1.17/level, feature_056 -1.55, feature_042 +1.10, feature_048 -0.98, feature_015 -0.58, feature_111 +0.53, feature_040 -0.47, all p<<0.001. Two top univariate candidates (feature_039, feature_034) lose significance after adjustment (p=0.32, 0.88). Model R^2 = 0.79 — these features alone explain ~80% of variance in pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a treatment-by-biomarker interaction: the positive effect of feature_042 on pfs_months is larger in patients with feature_015=0 than in patients with feature_015=1 (i.e., feature_015 attenuates the benefit of feature_042).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term coefficient = -1.62 (p ~ 4e-255). Cell means: feature_015=0,feature_042=0 -> 4.31 mo; feature_015=0,feature_042=1 -> 5.97 mo (gain +1.66 mo); feature_015=1,feature_042=0 -> 4.32; feature_015=1,feature_042=1 -> 4.35 (gain +0.03). feature_015 fully abolishes the feature_042 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_040 also abolishes the benefit of feature_042 on pfs_months: the interaction coefficient between feature_042 and feature_040 is strongly negative.
feature_111 shares the same biomarker dependence as feature_042: its positive effect on pfs_months is greatly reduced or abolished by feature_015=1 or feature_040=1.

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -1.36 (p ~ 2e-118). When feature_040=0, feature_042 adds +1.35 mo; when feature_040=1, feature_042 adds approximately 0 mo. feature_040 also abolishes the feature_042 benefit.

feature_111 x feature_015 interaction = -0.80 (p ~ 1e-56). feature_111 x feature_040 interaction = -0.66 (p ~ 4e-26). Same pattern: feature_111's univariate +0.57 mo benefit is concentrated in patients with feature_015=0 and feature_040=0; effect ~0 if either marker is positive.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the joint subgroup feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0, both feature_042 and feature_111 each individually carry their full positive effect on pfs_months, while in any subgroup with feature_015=1 OR feature_040=1, neither feature has detectable effect.

analyses.code: for s15,s40 in [(0,0),(0,1),(1,0),(1,1)]: stratified t-tests for feature_042 and feature_111

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the biomarker-negative subgroup (feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0), feature_042 and feature_111 are SYNERGISTIC rather than additive: each by itself yields little benefit in this subgroup, but the combination (feature_042=1 AND feature_111=1) yields a much larger increase in pfs_months than either alone.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months in biomarker-negative subgroup by (feature_042, feature_111, feature_039): (0,0,0) 4.32 (n=4503); (0,0,1) 4.25 (n=821); (0,1,0) 4.33 (n=1789); (0,1,1) 4.30 (n=10321); (1,0,0) 4.26 (n=2339); (1,0,1) 4.34 (n=416); (1,1,0) 7.02 (n=976); (1,1,1) 7.24 (n=5595). Both single agents alone give essentially zero benefit; combination feature_042+feature_111 gives ~+2.7 mo synergistic gain. Adding feature_039 to the combo gives a small additional ~+0.2 mo bump.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_039's apparent univariate benefit (+0.36 mo) is an artifact of co-administration with the feature_042+feature_111 combination; after adjustment for the combo, feature_039 has no independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Within combo+biomNeg subgroup, unadjusted feature_039 effect = +0.22 mo (p=0.052). Adjusting for age, feature_063, feature_056, feature_019 it falls to +0.13 mo (p=0.14). Within biomNeg patients NOT receiving the combo, feature_039 effect = -0.01 mo (p=0.69). feature_039 has no independent effect on pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The synergistic benefit of feature_042+feature_111 in biomarker-negative patients is invariant to age (feature_080): there is no significant interaction between age and the combo treatment.

analyses.result_summary: Combo x age interaction coefficient = -0.0016 (p=0.51). Combo benefit stays at ~+2.7 to +3.0 mo across age decades. By age bin within biom-negative subgroup: combo benefit (combo=1 vs 0) is approximately +2.6 (40s), +3.0 (50s), +2.9 (60s), +2.9 (70s), +3.0 (80s).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The combo treatment effect is invariant to disease severity (feature_063): the interaction between combo and feature_063 is not significant.

analyses.result_summary: Combo x feature_063 interaction = +0.016 (p=0.73). The combo benefit is similar in magnitude (~+2.9 mo) at each level of feature_063. No effect modification by disease severity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effects of feature_042, feature_111, and feature_039 do NOT vary by race (feature_011): patients of all racial categories receive similar mean PFS gains from each of these treatments.

analyses.code: for tx in [...]: for r in df['feature_011'].unique(): t-test in subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect by race: white +1.11, asian +1.06, black +1.06, hispanic +1.12, other +1.01 (all p<0.001 except 'other' p=7.4e-10). feature_111 effect by race: white +0.55, asian +0.69, black +0.64, hispanic +0.56, other +0.48. feature_039 effect by race: white +0.33, asian +0.48, black +0.56, hispanic +0.31, other +0.28. Effects are remarkably uniform across racial categories.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effects of feature_042, feature_111, and feature_039 do NOT vary by insurance type (feature_089): medicaid, medicare, private, and uninsured patients receive similar PFS gains from each treatment.

analyses.code: for tx: for ins in df['feature_089'].unique(): t-test in subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_042 effect by insurance: medicaid +1.08, private +1.09, medicare +1.11, uninsured +1.10 (all highly significant). feature_111 effect: medicaid +0.60, private +0.59, medicare +0.53, uninsured +0.66. feature_039 effect: medicaid +0.30, private +0.36, medicare +0.38, uninsured +0.42. Effects uniform across insurance types.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Race (feature_011) has no detectable main effect on pfs_months in this cohort: mean PFS is similar across white, asian, black, hispanic, and other categories.

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA F=0.85, p=0.494. Mean pfs_months: asian 4.74, black 4.72, hispanic 4.71, other 4.76, white 4.68. Differences are within ~0.08 mo of overall mean — no race main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Insurance type (feature_089) has no detectable main effect on pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: One-way ANOVA F=0.31, p=0.818. Mean pfs_months: medicaid 4.71, medicare 4.69, private 4.68, uninsured 4.73. Differences are within ~0.05 mo — no insurance main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5, mean ~3.8 — pattern consistent with serum albumin g/dL) is positively associated with pfs_months even after adjusting for age, stage, and combo treatment, but does NOT modify the treatment effect.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted slope of feature_019 = +0.48 mo per unit (p<<0.001). Combo x feature_019 interaction within biom-negative = -0.058 (p=0.39) — no effect modification. feature_019 acts as an independent prognostic factor (each 1 unit higher associated with +0.48 mo PFS), separately from treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_067 (continuous, mean ~3.8, range 0-24.6) is negatively associated with pfs_months as a prognostic marker; the combo treatment may be slightly more effective at higher feature_067, but the interaction is small relative to the main effects.

analyses.result_summary: Main slope of feature_067 = -0.081 mo/unit (p<<0.001). Combo x feature_067 interaction = +0.024 (p=0.006) — statistically significant but quantitatively very small. feature_067 is an independent negative prognostic marker; the combo's benefit increases by only ~0.024 mo per unit of feature_067, negligible clinically.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 and feature_040 are statistically independent of each other in this cohort (their joint distribution is consistent with the product of marginals).

analyses.result_summary: Chi-square = 0.86, p = 0.355. Observed counts: (0,0) 26760, (0,1) 5875, (1,0) 14180, (1,1) 3185 — closely match expected counts under independence. The two resistance markers occur independently.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 and feature_040 are PURELY PREDICTIVE, not prognostic: among untreated patients (no feature_042+feature_111 combination), neither marker is associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months among combo=0 patients: (feature_015=0,feature_040=0) 4.30; (0,1) 4.31; (1,0) 4.32; (1,1) 4.30. Differences <0.03 mo. feature_015 and feature_040 carry essentially zero prognostic information; their entire impact on pfs_months operates through abrogation of the combo treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_034 (binary, prevalence 37%) is mutually exclusive with feature_040 (no patient has both feature_034=1 and feature_040=1). Once feature_040 is accounted for, feature_034 has no independent effect on pfs_months.

analyses.code: pd.crosstab(df['feature_034'], df['feature_040']); ttest in biom-positive subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Cross-tab confirms: among feature_034=1 (n=18543), zero patients have feature_040=1; among feature_034=0 (n=31457), 9060 have feature_040=1. corr(feature_034,feature_040) = -0.36. In biomarker-positive subgroup, feature_034 PFS difference = -0.001 (p=0.98). Within combo+biomNeg, feature_034 effect = -0.06 (p=0.46). feature_034 captures part of the same disease-classification information as feature_040 but with no independent prognostic or predictive value.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is NO alternative treatment combination among feature_042, feature_111, and feature_039 that provides a detectable PFS benefit in patients with feature_015=1 OR feature_040=1: in the biomarker-positive population, all 8 treatment cells have mean pfs_months of approximately 4.3 months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months in biomarker-positive subgroup by (feature_042, feature_111, feature_039): (0,0,0) 4.31; (0,0,1) 4.20; (0,1,0) 4.35; (0,1,1) 4.32; (1,0,0) 4.33; (1,0,1) 4.13; (1,1,0) 4.32; (1,1,1) 4.35. All means within 0.22 mo of each other; no combination provides benefit. Treatment resistance is comprehensive, not specific to one drug.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between age (feature_080) and pfs_months is essentially linear; adding a quadratic term provides only marginal improvement in fit.

analyses.result_summary: Linear age model R^2 = 0.4922; with quadratic term R^2 = 0.4932 (delta = 0.0010). Quadratic coefficient = +0.0006 mo/yr^2 (p<<0.001 due to sample size, but trivial in magnitude). The age-PFS relationship is functionally linear at +0.176 mo/yr across the full 30-90 yr range.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_063 (3-level prognostic ordinal) carries information independent of feature_056 and feature_048 — feature_056 and feature_048 prevalence is constant across feature_063 levels, indicating these three are non-overlapping prognostic factors.

analyses.result_summary: Prevalence of feature_056 by feature_063 levels: 0->0.301, 1->0.297, 2->0.303 (no association). Prevalence of feature_048: 0->0.102, 1->0.099, 2->0.109. In multivariable OLS all three retain large coefficients (feature_063 -1.17/level, feature_056 -1.55, feature_048 -0.97; all p<<0.001). They contribute orthogonal prognostic information.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A consolidated 8-feature OLS model — age (feature_080), stage-like ordinal (feature_063), prognostic markers (feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, feature_019, feature_101), and the combo treatment x biomarker-negative interaction (feature_042 * feature_111 * (feature_015=0 AND feature_040=0)) — explains the large majority of variance in pfs_months in this cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Final consolidated OLS model R^2 = 0.876. Coefficients: feature_080 +0.176/yr, feature_063 -1.17/level, feature_056 -1.55, feature_048 -0.97, feature_067 -0.077/unit, feature_019 +0.48/unit, feature_101 -0.019/unit, combo_biomNeg +2.92 mo. All p<<0.001. The single combo-biomarker interaction term captures the entire treatment effect cleanly.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across all 127 features, after multivariable adjustment the major drivers of pfs_months reduce to: (a) age (feature_080, +0.18 mo/yr), (b) a graded severity marker (feature_063, -1.2 mo/level), (c) two independent dichotomous prognostic markers (feature_056 -1.55, feature_048 -0.97), (d) two laboratory-like prognostic continuous markers (feature_019 +0.48/unit, feature_067 -0.08/unit, feature_101 -0.02/unit), and (e) a single therapeutic interaction: combination of feature_042+feature_111 in biomarker-negative patients (+2.9 mo). Race and insurance have null main and interaction effects.

analyses.code: Synthesis across all prior analyses; comparison of consolidated model R^2 to simple univariate R^2.
analyses.result_summary: Univariate age R^2 = 0.49; adding the consolidated set raises R^2 to 0.876 (incremental 0.39 from non-age factors). The treatment landscape is binary: in biomarker-doubly-negative patients (54% of cohort), receiving feature_042+feature_111 jointly extends pfs_months by ~2.9 mo over no-combo; in biomarker-positive patients (46% of cohort, with feature_015=1 OR feature_040=1) NO combination of available treatments produces benefit. Demographic factors (race, insurance) show no main or modifier effect. Hypotheses h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, h7, h8, h9, h10, h11, h17, h18, h19, h20, h21, h22, h23, h24, h25, h26, h27, h28 SUPPORTED. Hypothesis h12 SUPPORTED (the null) — feature_039 has no independent effect once combo is adjusted for. Hypotheses h13, h14, h15, h16 SUPPORTED (the nulls) — no effect modification by age, stage, race, or insurance on combo benefit.
